examples.title: FCFS Scheduling Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FCFS_Schedule(processes[])
BEGIN
  Sort processes by arrival time
  currentTime = 0
  totalWaitingTime = 0
  
  FOR each process in processes DO
    IF currentTime < process.arrivalTime THEN
      currentTime = process.arrivalTime
    END IF
    
    waitingTime = currentTime - process.arrivalTime
    totalWaitingTime = totalWaitingTime + waitingTime
    
    currentTime = currentTime + process.burstTime
    process.completionTime = currentTime
    process.turnaroundTime = process.completionTime - process.arrivalTime
  END FOR
  
  averageWaitingTime = totalWaitingTime / numberOfProcesses
  RETURN averageWaitingTime
END
examples.explanation: FCFS sorts processes by arrival time and executes them in order without preemption. Each process waits for all previous processes to complete.

examples.title: SJF (Non-Preemptive) Scheduling Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Shortest Job First non-preemptive scheduling
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SJF_Schedule(processes[])
BEGIN
  completed = 0
  currentTime = 0
  n = number of processes
  isCompleted[n] = FALSE
  
  WHILE completed < n DO
    shortestJob = -1
    minimumBurst = INFINITY
    
    FOR i = 0 to n-1 DO
      IF isCompleted[i] = FALSE AND processes[i].arrivalTime <= currentTime THEN
        IF processes[i].burstTime < minimumBurst THEN
          minimumBurst = processes[i].burstTime
          shortestJob = i
        END IF
      END IF
    END FOR
    
    IF shortestJob == -1 THEN
      currentTime = currentTime + 1
    ELSE
      waitingTime = currentTime - processes[shortestJob].arrivalTime
      currentTime = currentTime + processes[shortestJob].burstTime
      processes[shortestJob].completionTime = currentTime
      processes[shortestJob].turnaroundTime = currentTime - processes[shortestJob].arrivalTime
      isCompleted[shortestJob] = TRUE
      completed = completed + 1
    END IF
  END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: At each step, SJF selects the ready process with the smallest burst time. It minimizes average waiting time but requires knowledge of all burst times.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²) - searches through all processes for each selection

examples.title: Round Robin Scheduling Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Round Robin preemptive scheduling with time quantum
examples.code:
ALGORITHM RoundRobin_Schedule(processes[], quantum)
BEGIN
  queue = empty
  currentTime = 0
  remainingBurst[n] = processes[].burstTime
  
  // Add all processes arriving at time 0
  FOR each process with arrivalTime = 0 DO
    enqueue(queue, process)
  END FOR
  
  WHILE queue is not empty DO
    currentProcess = dequeue(queue)
    
    IF remainingBurst[currentProcess] > quantum THEN
      currentTime = currentTime + quantum
      remainingBurst[currentProcess] = remainingBurst[currentProcess] - quantum
    ELSE
      currentTime = currentTime + remainingBurst[currentProcess]
      remainingBurst[currentProcess] = 0
      processes[currentProcess].completionTime = currentTime
    END IF
    
    // Add new arrivals during execution
    FOR each process that arrives during currentTime DO
      enqueue(queue, process)
    END FOR
    
    // Re-add if not completed
    IF remainingBurst[currentProcess] > 0 THEN
      enqueue(queue, currentProcess)
    END IF
  END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Round Robin uses a FIFO queue and time quantum. Each process executes for at most quantum time, then moves to back of queue if not complete.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × burst/quantum) - number of iterations depends on quantum size
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for queue and remaining burst array

examples.title: Priority Scheduling (Non-Preemptive) Implementation
examples.description: Implementation of priority-based scheduling where higher priority number means higher priority
examples.code:
def priority_schedule(processes):
    n = len(processes)
    completed = 0
    current_time = 0
    is_completed = [False] * n
    
    while completed < n:
        highest_priority = -1
        highest_priority_num = -1
        
        for i in range(n):
            if not is_completed[i] and processes[i][2] <= current_time:
                if processes[i][1] > highest_priority_num:
                    highest_priority_num = processes[i][1]
                    highest_priority = i
        
        if highest_priority == -1:
            current_time += 1
        else:
            # Execute the process
            waiting_time = current_time - processes[highest_priority][2]
            current_time += processes[highest_priority][3]
            processes[highest_priority].append(current_time)  # completion time
            is_completed[highest_priority] = True
            completed += 1
    
    return processes

# Process format: [pid, priority, arrival, burst]
# processes = [[1, 2, 0, 5], [2, 1, 1, 3], [3, 3, 2, 8]]
examples.explanation: Priority scheduling selects the highest priority process from ready queue. Ties are broken by arrival time (FCFS). Can suffer from starvation.
